title: Guzmania fastuosa
intro: Guzmania fastuosa, also known as the Queen of the Jungle or Fastuosa Bromeliad, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Guzmania fastuosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Guzmania fastuosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Guzmania fastuosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This family comprises a large and diverse group of tropical plants. Guzmania fastuosa is a member of the genus Guzmania, which includes around 120 species of epiphytes native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Bromeliad family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Guzmania fastuosa belonging to the subfamily Tillandsioideae. This classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this plant species.
